studyGuide.title: Introduction
studyGuide.content: Power Sharing means dividing the government's power among different groups so that no single person becomes a powerful dictator.

studyGuide.title: Why is it Important?
studyGuide.content: Sharing power stops fights between different religions, languages, or castes. It keeps the entire country peaceful and united.

studyGuide.title: Horizontal Power Sharing
studyGuide.content: Power is split evenly sideways: **Legislature** (makes laws), **Executive** (enforces laws), and **Judiciary** (courts).

studyGuide.title: Vertical Power Sharing
studyGuide.content: Power is shared downwards like a ladder: from the big Central Government down to the State Government, and then the local Village Panchayat.

studyGuide.title: Sri Lanka vs Belgium
studyGuide.content: Sri Lanka refused to share power, leading to a deadly civil war. Belgium shared power wonderfully, leading to peace!

studyGuide.title: Important Points
studyGuide.content:
• It is the true spirit and soul of a democracy
• It prevents large majorities from bullying small minorities
• Sometimes political parties join together to share a 'Coalition' government.
